variables.name: spacing/structure/XL
variables.description: Large Section Spacing. Used for significant vertical gaps between major page sections. Mapped to space[4] (32px) on desktop, scaling down to space[3] (24px) on mobile.

variables.name: spacing/structure/L
variables.description: Standard Section Spacing. Used for separating distinct content blocks within a section. Mapped to space[3] (24px) on desktop, scaling to space[2] (16px) on mobile.

variables.name: spacing/component/gap/L
variables.description: Primary Component Gap. Defines the internal spacing between large child elements within a component (e.g., card body to footer). Mapped to space[2] (16px).

variables.name: spacing/component/inset/XL
variables.description: Generous Component Padding. Used for the inner padding of large containers or hero-style cards. Scales from space[4] (32px) down to space[2.5] (20px) for mobile.

variables.name: spacing/component/inset/L
variables.description: Standard Component Padding. The default inner padding for cards and containers. Scales from space[3] (24px) down to space[2.5] (20px) for mobile.

variables.name: spacing/component/inset/M
variables.description: Compact Component Padding. Used for smaller cards, banners, or buttons. Fixed at space[2] (16px).

variables.name: spacing/structure/M
variables.description: Compact Section Spacing. Used for grouping related modules or headers. Consistently mapped to space[2] (16px) across most modes.

variables.name: spacing/component/gap/M
variables.description: Secondary Component Gap. Used for standard spacing between elements like icons and labels. Mapped to space[1] (8px).

variables.name: spacing/component/gap/S
variables.description: Tertiary Component Gap. Used for very tight internal spacing or subtle alignments. Mapped to space[0.5] (4px).

variables.name: spacing/component/inset/S
variables.description: Tight Component Padding. Used for small UI elements like tags or compact buttons. Fixed at space[1] (8px).

variables.name: spacing/component/inset/XS
variables.description: Minimal Component Padding. Used for the smallest interactive elements or tooltips. Fixed at space[0.5] (4px).

variables.name: spacing/micro/micro
variables.description: Atomic Spacing. The smallest possible unit in the system. Used for hairline alignments or extreme precision. Mapped to space[0.5] (4px).

variables.name: spacing/structure/S
variables.description: Tight Section Spacing. Used for small vertical gaps between related text elements. Fixed at space[1] (8px) across all modes.

variables.name: layout/breakpoint
variables.description: Responsive Viewport Target. Defines the specific screen width threshold for triggering layout transitions. Based on values: sm (390), md (734), lg (1024), and xl (1440).

variables.name: layout/grid-columns
variables.description: Vertical Content Subdivisions. The number of columns used to align content within the grid. This typically scales from 4 columns on mobile (sm) to 12 columns on desktop (lg/xl) to facilitate complex layouts.

variables.name: layout/grid-margin
variables.description: Outer Page Gutters. The horizontal safety margin between the content container and the edge of the viewport. Scales from 20px (sm) to 120px (xl) to maintain visual balance.

variables.name: layout/grid-gutter
variables.description: Inter-column Spacing. The fixed horizontal space between grid columns used to separate content modules. Ensures consistent internal alignment across all responsive modes.

variables.name: layout/content-max-width
variables.description: Container Limit. The maximum width the main content area can reach (1200px). It remains fluid on sm/md/lg and becomes fixed and centered on XL screens to prevent excessive line lengths.
